Package BusinessLayer

Class Pin

java.lang.Object
BusinessLayer.Pin
public class Pin extends Object
Represents an input or output pin on a digital component. Pins have a position on the canvas, a boolean value, a type (INPUT or OUTPUT), and can be connected to wires (Connectors). They belong to a specific Component. Pins are used to transfer boolean values between components via connectors. Each pin maintains a list of connected wires for simulation and rendering.

  • Constructor Details

    • Pin

      public Pin(int x, int y, Pin.PinType type, Component owner)
      Constructs a Pin at the given position, with type and owner component.
      Parameters:
      x - the x-coordinate of the pin
      y - the y-coordinate of the pin
      type - the type of pin (INPUT or OUTPUT)
      owner - the component this pin belongs to

  • Method Details

    • getValue

      public boolean getValue()
      Gets the current boolean value of the pin.
      Returns:
      true if HIGH (1), false if LOW (0)

    • setValue

      public void setValue(boolean value)
      Sets the boolean value of the pin.
      Parameters:
      value - true for HIGH, false for LOW

    • getX

      public int getX()
      Gets the x-coordinate of the pin.
      Returns:
      x-coordinate

    • getY

      public int getY()
      Gets the x-coordinate of the pin.
      Returns:
      x-coordinate

    • updatePosition

      public void updatePosition(int x, int y)
      Updates the pin position on the canvas.
      Parameters:
      x - new x-coordinate
      y - new y-coordinate

    • getType

      public Pin.PinType getType()
      Gets the type of the pin (INPUT or OUTPUT).
      Returns:
      pin type

    • getOwner

      public Component getOwner()
      Gets the owner component of this pin.
      Returns:
      the component this pin belongs to

    • addWire

      public void addWire(Connector wire)
      Adds a connector (wire) to this pin. Ensures no duplicate connections are added.
      Parameters:
      wire - the connector to attach

    • removeWire

      public void removeWire(Connector wire)
      Removes a connector (wire) from this pin.
      Parameters:
      wire - the connector to remove

    • getConnectedWires

      public ArrayList<Connector> getConnectedWires()
      Returns a list of all connectors connected to this pin.
      Returns:
      a copy of the list of connected wires

    • isConnected

      public boolean isConnected()
      Checks if the pin has any connections.
      Returns:
      true if connected to at least one wire

    • distanceTo

      public double distanceTo(int px, int py)
      Calculates the Euclidean distance from this pin to a given point. Useful for snapping wires to pins.
      Parameters:
      px - x-coordinate of the point
      py - y-coordinate of the point
      Returns:
      distance to the point

    • clonePin

      public Pin clonePin(Component newOwner)
      Creates and returns a copy of this pin for a new owner component.
      Parameters:
      newOwner - the new owner component
      Returns:
      a new Pin with the same type, position, and value
